title: × cornoathyrium cornopteroides
parts: × cornoathyrium cornopteroides has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and stems, which contain various bioactive compounds. The roots of this plant are rich in iridoid glycosides, including cornopterine, which may possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of × cornoathyrium cornopteroides contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have been reported to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The stems of this plant may also contain sesquiterpenes, including cornopterone, which may possess immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects.
